questions.text: Does Store manager address safety at the morning assembly or monthly meeting etc?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show an example which store manager addressed safety at the morning assembly or monthly meeting etc.

questions.text: Do you implement BS Safety fundamental Activities (3S, KY, RA and Safety rules) regularly?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can provide one example of activity in each 4 fundamental activities. (e.g. for 3S - cleaning plan, inspection of respect of 3S visual standard; for KY: KYT, self check by teammates before acting; for RA: training on risks, verification of understanding of risks; for SR : verify that the safety rules are displayed in the shop and confirm that Teammates are familiar with them.)

questions.text: Do you review site H&S performances results and take any actions against identified opportunities for improvements?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain what actions have been taken to improve H&S performances.

questions.text: Does the Store Management regularly involve all teammates with safety discussions and incorporate their idea into safety action?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show an example that he/she involved all teammates with safety discussions and incorporated their idea into safety action.

questions.text: Are all teammates provided with PPEs needed for their tasks?
questions.judgement_criteria: Assessor can confirm that teammates have PPE on site. Or respondent can show where the PPEs are stored.

questions.text: Are all teammates are involved with 3S activities?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show an example that all teammates are involved 3S activities.

questions.text: Do you comfortable reporting safety issues to your store manager?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain his/her exampled experience which made him/her feeling comfortable reporting. (e.g. Store manager improved the safety issues soon after reported)

questions.text: Do you know how to report incidents?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain correct way to report incidents.

questions.text: Are you aware of residual or high risks and inform teammates?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show an example of residual or high risk. He/she can explain how to inform it to teammates.

questions.text: Are teammates corrected when not following Safety Rules? And are you leading by example?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can describe an example that he/she corrected teammate who didn't follow Safety rules. *Record/evidence is not required.

questions.text: Are you aware of required PPE to use?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain correct requires PPE.

questions.text: Do you know how to report incidents?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain correct way to report incident.

questions.text: Are teammates aware of recent safety incidents, if any.
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain recent safety incident. If there is no incident, it is ok to answer that.

questions.text: Are you actively participating in safety discussions.
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show an example that he/she actively participated in safety discussions.

questions.text: Have you reported unsafe conditions when identified?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show an example of unsafe condition he/she reported and manager's responses.

questions.text: Have you intervened to warn your teammate or reported when unsafe behavior is identified?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent states that he/she has warned the teammate or has reported when identified.

questions.text: Teammates ensure step stools or ladders are in good condition before using.
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain what should be done before using and can explain how to do if they are not in good condition.

questions.text: Are machine, power tools and hand tools inspected before using to ensure they are in good condition.
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain what should be done before using and can explain how to do if they are not in good condition.

questions.text: Have teammates proposed safety improvements to prevent injuries or near misses.
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show an example that he/she proposed safety improvements to prevent injuries or near misses.

questions.text: Do you work safely even when busy?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can give example he/she was very busy but nonetheless gave priority to safety.

questions.text: Do all teammates participate in safety activities?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show an example that all teammates participate in safety activities. (e.g. All TMs participate in group to perform KY training)

questions.text: You allocate time for teammates to perform 3S activities.
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain when teammates perform 3S activities.

questions.text: Do you know how to access safety procedures and standards?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain how to access safety procedures and standards.

questions.text: Do you know the Rules of Safety that are required to be followed?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show an example of safety rules, procedures or working standards that are required to be followed.

questions.text: You recognize Teammate when they practice safe behaviors.
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain how he/she check if teammates practice safe behaviors.

questions.text: What do you do for your safety when you encounter an abnormal situation?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain that he/her interrupts the service and communicates with leadership or colleague(s) in case of an abnormal situation.

questions.text: Do you communicate safety messages to teammates to improve their safety awareness?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show an example of his/her safety messages to teammate to improve their safety awareness. (e.g. Top management message, own experiences to prevent incidents)

questions.text: Do you feel comfortable stopping work if you feel there is a risk of injury.
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain why they feel comfortable stopping work if you feel there is a risk of injury.

questions.text: Do you maintain the skill qualification list/table including vehicle elevation/lowering operation?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show the latest skill qualification list/table including vehicle elevating/lowering.

questions.text: Routine Lift inspections are completed by outside vendor?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show that routine lift inspections are completed by outside vendor with inspection record.

questions.text: Are proper lifting equipment used (under load capacity) and are lifting arms set at appropriate position of vehicle?
questions.judgement_criteria: Safety operations on vehicle elevating/lowering are performed on site. (complied with safety rules for vehicle elevation/lowering operations)

questions.text: What do you check to ensure the lift is adapted for the vehicle you need to service?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain that he/she checks that the rated maximum load of the lift is suitable for the weight of the vehicle to be services, including any load in the vehicle.

questions.text: Do you observe teammates to ensure they follow lifting procedure?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain how to observe store teammates to ensure lifting procedures are being followed

questions.text: Do you maintain the list of teammates who have legal licenses and/or legal training certifications required to perform the services including tire inflation, driving vehicle/forklift.
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show that teammates have required legal licenses and/or legal training records.

questions.text: Are prevention measures taken to ensure no person, objects, or body parts are in the danger zone when inflating tires? (ex. Use safety gauge/safety net, Keep enough distance etc.)
questions.judgement_criteria: Prevention measures are taken to ensure no person, objects, or body parts are in the danger zone when inflating tires. *Refer to examples described in question.

questions.text: How do you determine the inflation pressure needed for the tyres you are inflating?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show that he/she knows how to determine the recommended manufacturer pressure for a tyre and he/she does not inflate tires beyond that pressure.

questions.text: Do you always inspect the tire and the rim for signs of deterioration before inflating?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain the steps for preliminary inspection before inflating a tire.

questions.text: Do you check frequently the tire during inflation and for what signs?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain the procedure to checks for unexpected bulges or defects when inflating a tire.

questions.text: Are you aware of the proper lifting techniques?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain the proper lifting techniques.

questions.text: Are paths kept clear to allow for material movement in the shop area?
questions.judgement_criteria: Paths are kept clear to allow for material movement in the shop area on site.

questions.text: What are the rules for storing tyres to avoid risk of fall or ergonomic risks?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show that e.g. they access rack and shelves in safety (e.g. with platform ladder, or with forklift), that tyres on shelves and racks do not pose a risk of fall, that the max. loads indicated on storage system is respected, that stacks of tyres are no higher than shoulder's height, that large tyre/wheels (>200kg) are not left upright without falling prevention measures.

questions.text: Do teammates use mechanical aids when handling heavy objects (e.g. rim, oil drums) or tyre/wheel assembly or alternatively ask for assistance?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show an example that teammates are using mechanical aids or ask for assistance when handling on site.

questions.text: Are heavier items or frequently used items stored at mid-body height to make them easier to reach?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show that heavier items or frequently used items are stored at mid-body height to make them easier to reach by site observation.

questions.text: What is the rule to prevent the injury caused by overturning forklift? (question to teammate who may drive forklift)
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain one and more safety rules on forklifts. (e.g. fasten seatbelt, avoid raising or lowering forks while the forklift is in motion)

questions.text: Are vehicles parked at a location where the task can be conducted in a safe condition? (e.g. flat surface, wheel chocks, cones used, away from traffic).
questions.judgement_criteria: Vehicles are parked at a location where the task can be conducted in a safe condition on site. *Refer to examples described in question.

questions.text: What are the rules when driving a car for test drive and/or road service?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondents say that e.g. he/she wear seatbelts, doesn't use cell phone and follows local law when operating vehicles.

questions.text: What are the rules applicable when guiding a vehicle in the store premises?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain examples to comply. (e.g. maintain a safe position away from the moving vehicle (stay visible etc), guide the driver to move safely the vehicle using hand/arm and/or voice signals, keep attention on the teammates in the store)

questions.text: Do teammates complete an inspection to confirm safe operation of the vehicle before driving?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain the way teammates complete an inspection to confirm safe operation of the vehicle before driving.

questions.text: When you perform vehicle maintenance services, what do you do to prevent injury caused by rotating/moving part of vehicle?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain that intervention next to rotating/moving part shall be avoided, engine shall be switched off for maintenance, and JHA/JSA/KY must be conducted and safety measures are in place when engine cannot be off.

questions.text: Do Rotating equipment or objects under mechanical compression or tension have guarding/barriers? (e.g. tire balance MC, springs, shock absorber machine, brake lathes, bearing press, grinders, etc.).
questions.judgement_criteria: Rotating equipment or objects under mechanical compression or tension have guarding/barriers on site.

questions.text: Are the right tools used for specific tasks and in good condition?
questions.judgement_criteria: Teammates use the right tools for specific tasks and the tools are in good condition on site.

questions.text: Equipment that is unsafe and requires maintenance or repair is immediately taken out of service.
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent indicates that unsafe equipment, or requiring maintenance or repair are immediately taken out of service and signed as out of order.

questions.text: What do you do for your safety when you have any doubt during maintenance service on EV?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain the way to verify proper safety steps on EV service. (e.g. check the service manual for EV.)

questions.text: Does Leader to GENBA operation discuss Safety at regularly scheduled meetings?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain when regularly meeting is held. He/she can show an example of safety discussion at the meeting.

questions.text: Have Leader to GENBA operation communicated measurable expectations for safety performance?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show an example that he/she communicated measurable expectations for safety performance.

questions.text: Do you make effort in order that safety is aligned with other business values such as revenue, margin and cost reduction?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain that how they make effort in order that safety is aligned with other business values such as revenue, margin and cost reduction. For example, he/she monitors and reports safety KPIs along with revenue, margin and cost KPI.

questions.text: Do you make effort to allocate support and resources for implementing safety programs and activities?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show an example of their effort to allocate support and resources for implementing safety programs and activities.

questions.text: Are supervisors and Leaders to GENBA operation trained to use "On the spot coaching" when a safety risk is identified?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain detail of the training, when and what supervisors and Leader to GENBA operation are trained to use "On the spot coaching" when a safety risk is identified.

questions.text: Are Best Practices collected and used to improve the Safety Program?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show an example to collect Best Practices and improve the Safety Program.

questions.text: Is the effectiveness of the Site Safety Rules reviewed after incidents and audits?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show an example that the effectiveness of the Site Safety Rules reviewed after incidents and audits.

questions.text: Does Leader to GENBA operation use leading & lagging metrics to guide continuous improvement efforts and decision-making?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show an example to guide continuous improvement efforts and decision-making with use of leading & lagging metrics.

questions.text: Are safety goals reviewed/revised regularly and used in business planning?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show their safety goals and business planning. Assessor can confirm that safety goals are reviewed/revised regularly and used in business planning.

questions.text: Are responsibilities for Safety on each positions (Leaders to Genba operation, Supervisors and Teammates) defined?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show the definitions of responsibilities for safety on each positions.

questions.text: Is Safety Orientation/Induction Training required for new Teammates? Can you describe the process?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show that safety orientation/induction training for new teammates is conducted with the guideline or training record etc.

questions.text: Do Teammates receive training in a language and at a literacy level that all of them can understand?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain which language are used for teammates' training with the training material etc. All teammates can understand the language at a literacy level.

questions.text: Is there a formal safety training program for Teammates, including  supervisors and  Leaders to GENBA operation?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show the formal safety training program for Teammates, including supervisors and Leaders to GENBA operation.

questions.text: Are safety trends or analytics shared with Teammates?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show an example of safety trends or analytics* shared with Teammates. *e.g. He/she shared information to call teammates' attention. "Recently, slip/trip accidents have occurred frequently."

questions.text: Are Teammates informed of their personal responsibility for a safe workplace?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain how teammate are informed of their personal responsibility for a safe workplace. (e.g. BSFA and compliance of store rule are informed at orientation/induction training for new teammates. They are reminded by e-learning annually.)

questions.text: Are Teammates trained on how to report an injury, illness, hazard, or concern, including near misses?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain how to train teammates on how to report an injury, illness, hazard, or concern, including near misses with the guideline or training record etc.

questions.text: Does Leader to GENBA operation seek Teammate input on hazard reduction and safety improvement?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain how to seek Teammate input on hazard reduction and safety improvement by Leader to GENBA operation.

questions.text: Has the Bridgestone Safety Mission Statement been communicated to all Teammates?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain how the Bridgestone Safety Mission Statement is communicated to all teammates with the guideline or communication record etc.

questions.text: Are cross-functional teams used to address specific safety and health concerns?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show an example that cross-functional teams* were used to address specific safety and health concerns. *e.g. other departments in your local company, other local companies

questions.text: Is there a recognition program for teammates that demonstrate safe behaviors?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show an example of the recognition program* for teammates that demonstrate safe behaviors. *e.g. Safety awards for performing above expectation

questions.text: Are relevant Safety Procedures available to all Teammates?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain the way all teammate access to relevant Safety Procedures.

questions.text: Is Safety Training delivered by qualified trainers?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain that safety training is delivered by qualified trainers with the guideline or training record etc.

questions.text: Are Near Miss and Lesson Learned Reports reviewed and used to improve the Safety Program?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show an example that Near Miss and Lesson Learned Reports are reviewed and used to improve the Safety Program.

questions.text: Are teammates provided adequate safety equipment? Do they know how to use them?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain that adequate safety equipment is provided and that teammates are trained on the usage with an example.

questions.text: Is there a an assessment program with scheduled compliance and systems audits?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show the assessment program with scheduled compliance and systems audits.

questions.text: Is the safety training curriculum tailored to the specific roles and responsibilities at each level of the organization?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain that the safety training curriculum is tailored to the specific roles and responsibilities at each level of the organization.

questions.text: Is a Risk Assessment conducted for new projects, materials and work methods?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show a sample of RA conducted for new projects, materials and work methods.

questions.text: Are certifications and training required by government regulations tracked to ensure completion?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain how to trace the completion of the certifications and training required by government regulations with the guideline or training record etc.

questions.text: Have important documents and records that need to be kept been identified? All documents that are legally required are kept.
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show the guideline or rule etc. to identify important documents/records and to keep them. If safety dept. have to keep some documents, assessor can confirm that all of them are kept on site.

questions.text: Are regulatory and corporate compliance systematically checked to ensure they are up-to-date?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain that regulatory and corporate compliance are systematically checked to ensure they are up-to-date with check record etc.

questions.text: Is there an established emergency policy/procedure that complies with regulations?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show the established emergency policy/procedure that complies with regulations.

questions.text: Are hazards associated with emergencies and non-routine operations identified in the emergency action plan and operating procedures, respectively?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show that hazards associated with emergencies and non-routine operations are identified in the emergency action plan and operating procedures, respectively.

questions.text: Are Emergency Plans written, communicated, revised, and facility drills conducted? Does the program update based on the result of the drill?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show that emergency plans are written, communicated, revise. He/she can show that facility drills conducted with the drill record.

questions.text: Is all regulatory required safety training included in the safety training matrix/plan?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show that all regulatory required safety training is included in the safety training matrix/plan.

questions.text: Do job descriptions have safety specific responsibilities identified?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show that job descriptions have safety specific responsibilities identified.

questions.text: Is safety awareness communicated via activities such as newsletters, alerts, and toolbox talks, etc.?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show an example that safety awareness is communicated via activities such as newsletters, alerts, and toolbox talks, etc.

questions.text: Are applicable site safety rules and/or management processes provided to contractor?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show a sampled evidence that BS provided necessary safety information to contractors. (e.g. online program, email, training record)

questions.text: Is there a written Contractor Safety Program?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show the written Contractor Safety Program.

questions.text: Are safety control measures determined for the contractor's services reviewed with BS in advance?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show a sampled evidence that safety control measures were reviewed with BS. (e.g. meeting minutes, pre-work planning document)

questions.text: Is on site check performed before starting the contractor's services to ensure that the determined control measures are in place?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show the record of safety inspection/observation. (e.g. checksheet that is used before the start of service)

questions.text: Are there established policies/procedures describing safety expectations, roles, and responsibilities?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show the established policies/procedures describing safety expectations, roles, and responsibilities.

questions.text: Are risk assessments completed and communicated with teammates to identify job hazards?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show a sample of RA and identified job hazards. He/she can explain how to communicate them to teammates.

questions.text: Are corrective actions, with accountability, tracked to closure?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain the system to ensure that corrective actions, with accountability, are tracked to closure.

questions.text: Are controls selected according to the “hierarchy of controls,” emphasizing, in order of priority; elimination, substitution, engineering controls, administrative controls, and PPE?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can explain that controls are selected according to the “hierarchy of controls,” emphasizing, in order of priority with a sample of RA.

questions.text: Has a standard practice for Vehicle Elevating and Lowering Operation that includes all elements of the SBU Standard been developed and Implemented?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show that the standard practice for Vehicle Elevating and Lowering Operation that includes all elements of the SBU Standard has been developed and Implemented.

questions.text: Has a standard practice for Tire Inflation that includes all elements of the SBU Standard been developed and Implemented?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show that the standard practice for Tire Inflation that includes all elements of the SBU Standard has been developed and Implemented.

questions.text: Has a standard practice for Tire/Large Tire Manual Handling Operation that includes all elements of the SBU Standard been developed and Implemented?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show that the standard practice for Tire/Large Tire Manual Handling Operation that includes all elements of the SBU Standard has been developed and Implemented.

questions.text: Has a standard practice for Motor Vehicle Safety that includes all elements of the SBU Standard been developed and Implemented?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show that the standard practice for Motor Vehicle Safety that includes all elements of the SBU Standard has been developed and Implemented.

questions.text: Have controls been developed and implemented to prevent Mechanical Hazard risks?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show a sample of RA that controls have been developed and implemented to mitigate Mechanical Hazard risks.

questions.text: Is there a documented process for investigation and corrective action following a safety incident?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show the documented process for investigation and corrective action following a safety incident.

questions.text: Once safety controls installed, are they monitored to ensure that teammates understand their use and application and to verify that they are effective?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show the record to ensure that teammates understand safety controls' use and application and to verify that they are effective.

questions.text: Are interim controls adopted while permanent controls are being determined?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show an example that interim controls were adopted while permanent controls were being determined.

questions.text: Before making changes to operations, workflow/work methodology, physical plant/facility, equipment, or materials, is there a safety review to identify any safety or health issues?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show the record of the safety review to identify any safety or health issues before making changes to operations, workflow/work methodology, physical plant/facility, equipment, or materials.

questions.text: Do teammates receive supplemental training when a change in the workplace could introduce new or increased hazards?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show that teammates receive supplemental training when a change in the workplace could introduce new or increased hazards with the guideline or training record etc.

questions.text: Are leading indicators used to review and analyze safety policies, procedures and trends for continual improvement?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show an example that leading indicators are used to review and analyzing safety policies, procedures and trends for continual improvement

questions.text: Are performance indicators used to track progress toward safety program goals?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show an example that performance indicators are used to track progress toward safety program goals.

questions.text: Are targeted safety campaigns/initiatives used to improve performance?
questions.judgement_criteria: Respondent can show an example of targeted safety campaigns/initiatives used to improve performance.
